name: Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop
name_original: Subida a las Lagunas de Neila por el Puerto El Collado — circular por el Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila
description: Cycle the challenging Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop, a 26.2-mile route through scenic forests and past glacial lakes in Spain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Palacios De La Sierra traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and climbs to high-altitude lagoons. The region features a mix of dense pine forests and open areas, offering varied scenery for road cyclists. Routes often connect small towns and villages, providing a glimpse into the local environment. Best road cycling routes around Palacios De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route features a notable climb towards the scenic Lagunas de Neila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Quintanar de la Sierra – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Aparcamiento para turismos, a difficult 46.3 miles (74.5 km) path. This route connects two towns through varied terrain, including ascents and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Quintanar de la Sierra – Direction Puerto El Collado loop from Quintanar de la Sierra, a 51.3 miles (82.5 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Palacios De La Sierra is defined by challenging mountain passes, high-altitude lagoons, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with over 50 routes available. The routes in Palacios De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Palacios De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level for road cycling in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: Road cycling in Palacios De La Sierra is characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and climbs to high-altitude lagoons. The region offers a range of routes, with 23 classified as moderate and 24 as difficult, making it suitable for cyclists looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: While many routes feature challenging climbs, there are a few options suitable for beginners. The region offers 3 routes classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to road cycling amidst the scenic pine forests and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Palacios De La Sierra?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape featuring dense pine forests, open areas, and stunning views as you climb towards high-altitude lagoons. Routes often connect small towns and villages, offering glimpses into the local environment and culture.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Palacios De La Sierra are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056054433/lagunas-de-neila-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons loop from Quintanar de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural attractions I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can cycle past the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2010732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, which are a highlight for many. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Laguna (2004m)&lt;/a&gt; and the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Leonardo de Yagüe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056054433/lagunas-de-neila-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 42.2 km, typically takes around 2 hours 19 minutes, while longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Soria – Molinos de Duero loop&lt;/a&gt; (104.4 km) can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the beauty of the high-altitude lagoons, and the extensive pine forests that define the region's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that connect different towns or villages?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to connect local towns and villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanar de la Sierra – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Aparcamiento para turismos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that links two towns through varied terrain, offering a great way to explore the local area.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: Given the region's significant elevation changes and mountain passes, late spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions for road cycling. During these months, you can expect more stable weather and accessible routes, avoiding potential snow or ice at higher altitudes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Palacios De La Sierra?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Palacios De La Sierra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there opportunities for scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The routes often lead to high-altitude areas and mountain passes, providing numerous opportunities for breathtaking scenic viewpoints, especially around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2010732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; and other elevated sections of the trails.

Q: What should I consider bringing for a road cycling trip in this region?
A: Due to the significant elevation changes and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring appropriate gear for mountain cycling, including layers of clothing for changing weather conditions, sufficient water and snacks, and a repair kit. A GPS device or a loaded komoot tour is also recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: The full loop, as described, is approximately 42 kilometers (26 miles) long with an elevation gain of about 866 meters. It typically takes around 2 hours and 19 minutes of active cycling time to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the overall tour is rated as moderate, the climb to Lagunas de Neila itself is considered highly difficult, often categorized as a Category 1 climb. It features sustained steep gradients, especially in the final kilometers, with sections reaching 11-17%. This route requires very good fitness due to its demanding nature.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features generally good road conditions with minimal traffic. The initial sections from Quintanar de la Sierra might include some concrete before transitioning to well-maintained asphalt. You'll experience a mix of open rolling hills and dense pine forests, with challenging steep ascents and some flatter or downhill parts.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque scenery throughout. Key highlights include the charming town of Quintanar de la Sierra, the stunning glacial Neila Lagoons themselves, and the challenging ascent towards Puerto El Collado. The climb from Villavelayo is particularly scenic, offering expansive views and following a stream.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails or paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the official cycling climbs, including parts of the [CIMA BU03] Lagunas de Neila * Quintanar de la Sierra / El Collado and [CIMA BU03] Lagunas de Neila * Quintanar de la Sierra (carretera vieja). You'll also briefly encounter sections of the Ruta del Sureste and other local paths.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more favorable, and the mountain passes are clear of snow. Summer offers warmer temperatures, but spring and autumn provide pleasant cycling conditions and vibrant natural colors.

faqs.question: Are there any specific permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits or entrance fees are required to ride this route or to access the Natural Park of the Glacial Lagoons of Neila. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation within the park.

faqs.question: Is the Lagunas de Neila Climb Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so bringing dogs along for the ride itself is generally not practical or safe. If you plan to visit the area with a dog and wish to explore on foot, dogs are usually allowed in the Natural Park on a leash, but always check specific regulations for any protected areas or trails you might want to hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: A common starting point for this loop is Quintanar de la Sierra, where you can find parking. This town serves as a good base for accessing the climb. Public transport options directly to the starting point might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach the area.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby for cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, towns like Quintanar de la Sierra offer various services for visitors. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options there, making it a convenient place to refuel and rest before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What makes the Lagunas de Neila climb particularly notable for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Lagunas de Neila climb is deeply ingrained in Spanish cycling history, known for its brutal final kilometers. It has been a decisive stage finish in prestigious professional races like the Vuelta a España and the Vuelta a Burgos, where it has seen victories from renowned cyclists. Beyond its professional pedigree, the natural beauty of the glacial lakes and surrounding forests within the Natural Park makes it a significant and rewarding destination for cycling enthusiasts.
